info.title: CSGHub Lite Local API
info.description:
Structured documentation for the local `/api/*` and `/v1/*` endpoints served by `csghub-lite`.

Maintenance note: whenever you add, remove, or rename an `/api/*` or `/v1/*` route in `internal/server/routes.go`, update this file and run the OpenAPI sync test before merging.

tags.name: Models
tags.description: Model listing, metadata, lifecycle, and download endpoints.

tags.name: Inference
tags.description: Local text generation and chat endpoints.

tags.name: Marketplace
tags.description: Proxy endpoints for listing models and datasets from CSGHub.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Local machine and application settings endpoints.

tags.name: Providers
tags.description: Third-party OpenAI-compatible provider configuration and model provider discovery.

tags.name: Upgrade
tags.description: Local application update checks and upgrade progress.

tags.name: CloudAuth
tags.description: OpenCSG access token status and management.

tags.name: Logs
tags.description: Server-side and AI App log streaming.

tags.name: AI Apps
tags.description: Managed AI App listing, install, launch, and shell endpoints.

get.description: Returns local, CSGHub cloud, and selected third-party provider models. Use `provider` to filter by a model provider ID such as `local`, `csghub`, or a third-party provider ID. Third-party provider filters return only models selected through `/api/tags/manage`; configured third-party names are accepted as legacy aliases.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: Force a refresh of the cloud model catalog before returning results.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Filter models by normalized provider ID. Local models use `local`; all CSGHub cloud models use `csghub`; third-party provider models use the configured provider ID. Configured third-party names are accepted as legacy aliases.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional csghub-lite category key from `/api/pipeline-tags`. When provided, only models whose pipeline tag belongs to the category are returned.

get.description: Returns csghub-lite model categories and the Hugging Face task / pipeline_tag values that belong to each category.

200.description: Pipeline tags grouped by csghub-lite category.

get.description: Returns all currently available models from a configured third-party provider. This catalog is used to choose which provider models appear in `/api/tags?provider=...`.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Third-party provider ID or configured provider name.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional csghub-lite category key from `/api/pipeline-tags`. When omitted, all provider catalog models are returned.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Third-party provider ID or configured provider name.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Third-party provider ID or configured provider name.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional csghub-lite category key from `/api/pipeline-tags`. When supplied, the response is filtered to selected models in that category.

patch.description: Updates metadata for a selected third-party provider model and can change the client-visible model ID. The original provider model ID is preserved internally for upstream inference calls. Use an empty string to clear an overridden display name or description.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Third-party provider ID or configured provider name.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Current client-visible provider model ID to update. Required when changing the model ID; the request body `model` value is the new client-visible ID.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Third-party provider ID or configured provider name.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Provider model ID to remove from the selected list.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive search text matched against model metadata.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Filter by local model format, such as `gguf`.

parameters.name: pipeline_tag
parameters.description: Filter by detected pipeline type, such as `text-generation` or `image-text-to-text`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to `20`, capped at `100`.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Zero-based offset into the filtered result set.

get.summary: Get a local model download manifest by public model ID
get.description: Resolves a local model by its public inference ID, such as a short model name shown in the local model library, and returns its download manifest.

200.description: Local model metadata with downloadable files.

get.summary: Get a local model download manifest

200.description: Local model metadata with downloadable files.

post.description: Imports a local model from multipart form data. Use `mode=archive` for a single `.zip`, `.tar`, `.tar.gz`, or `.tgz` file; use `mode=files` or `mode=directory` with one or more files and matching `paths` values.

model.description: Optional target model ID in `namespace/name` form. Defaults to `local/<filename>` when omitted.

paths.description: Relative paths corresponding to uploaded files. Folder uploads should provide browser-relative paths.

post.summary: Start a local model upload session
post.description: Creates a resumable-style upload session used by the web UI for large model files. Files are uploaded one at a time with raw PUT requests, then imported on complete.

put.summary: Upload a file to a model upload session

get.summary: Download a cached local model file
get.description: Downloads a cached model file and supports HTTP Range requests for resumable downloads. HEAD requests are also supported.

parameters.name: path...
parameters.description: Slash-separated relative path to the cached file within the model directory.

206.description: Partial file content for a range request.

post.description: Streams model download progress as Server-Sent Events.
Loads a model into memory and optionally streams progress while conversions are running.

200.description: Load completed or streaming progress returned.

post.description: Generates text from a prompt. By default the endpoint streams tokens as Server-Sent Events unless `stream` is set to `false`.

example.prompt: Write a haiku about local inference.

200.description: Generated text or a streaming event sequence.

post.description: Supports local models and, when `source` is `cloud`, OpenCSG-backed chat models. Streaming responses are sent as SSE when `Accept: text/event-stream` or `X-CSGHUB-Stream: sse` is provided; otherwise streaming defaults to NDJSON.

200.description: Chat completion output or streaming token events.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results (default: 20, max: 100)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip

get.summary: Get a local dataset download manifest

200.description: Local dataset metadata with downloadable files.

get.summary: Download a cached local dataset file
get.description: Downloads a cached dataset file and supports HTTP Range requests for resumable downloads. HEAD requests are also supported.

parameters.name: path...
parameters.description: Slash-separated relative path to the cached file within the dataset directory.

206.description: Partial file content for a range request.

post.summary: List files inside a dataset path

200.description: File and directory entries under the requested dataset path.

post.description: Streams dataset download progress as Server-Sent Events.

get.description: Proxies the upstream CSGHub model listing API and normalizes the response to `{ data, total }`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Marketplace sort mode such as `trending` or `recently_updated`.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Optional upstream source filter forwarded to CSGHub.

parameters.name: framework
parameters.description: Optional model type filter forwarded to CSGHub, such as `gguf` or `safetensors`.

get.description: Fetches a single marketplace model and, for GGUF repositories, summarizes the available quantization variants discovered in the upstream file tree.

200.description: Marketplace model details and GGUF quantization summary.

get.description: Proxies the upstream CSGHub dataset listing API and normalizes the response to `{ data, total }`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Marketplace sort mode such as `trending` or `recently_updated`.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Optional upstream source filter forwarded to CSGHub.

200.description: CPU, memory, and GPU information gathered from the local machine.
Minimal application settings surfaced to the UI.

post.description: Starts local text-to-image generation in the background and returns a job that can be polled for status and result.

202.description: Job is still queued or running.

get.description: Reports whether the lazy Diffusers runtime for local text-to-image generation is installed and ready.

post.description: Creates or repairs the user-scoped Python Diffusers runtime. This endpoint is intended to be called only when a user runs local text-to-image generation.

200.description: Browsable subdirectories and navigation roots for the requested directory.

get.description: Returns the remote access authorization setting and API key metadata. Secret values are never returned.

200.description: API key metadata and authorization state.

post.description: Creates an API key for remote access. The plaintext key is returned only once.

201.description: Created API key with one-time plaintext secret.

post.summary: Update local API key authorization settings
post.description: Enables or disables API key checks for non-loopback inference API requests. Localhost remains passwordless, and non-inference management endpoints are not protected by this setting.

get.description: Returns aggregate request and token usage grouped by API key, model, and source.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Usage time range. `week`, `month`, and `year` are rolling windows.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Optional provider name filter. Matches the recorded provider display name case-insensitively.

get.description: Returns providers available for `/api/tags?provider=...` filtering by default or when `source=model`. Use `source=third_party` to return configured third-party provider settings.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Provider list source. `model` returns model filter providers; `third_party` returns configured provider settings.

parameters.name: locale
parameters.description: Optional response locale. When set to `zh`, the built-in local provider name is returned in Chinese. Third-party provider names remain user-configured.

200.description: Provider list for the selected source.

201.description: Created provider without its API key.

post.description: Checks the provider base URL and API key by fetching its OpenAI-compatible `/models` endpoint. The provider is not saved.

200.description: Provider settings are valid and returned at least one model.
Updated provider without its API key.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query matched against conversation title, model, and message text.

200.description: Current version and latest release information when available.

post.description: Streams Server-Sent Events with check, download, extract, install, completion, or error status updates.

text/event-stream.example:
data: {"status":"checking","progress":0,"message":"Checking for updates..."}

data: {"status":"completed","progress":100,"message":"Successfully upgraded to 0.8.20. Please restart the application.","version":"0.8.20"}

200.description: Current access token status and, when available, the authenticated user profile.

post.summary: Save or replace the OpenCSG access token

200.description: Updated auth status after saving the token.

delete.summary: Clear the saved OpenCSG access token

200.description: Updated auth status after clearing the token.

post.summary: Save or replace the manual OpenCSG API Key

200.description: Updated auth status after saving the API key.

delete.summary: Clear the manual OpenCSG API Key

200.description: Updated auth status after clearing the API key.

get.description: Returns recent log history and then continues streaming new log lines as Server-Sent Events.

text/event-stream.example:
data: 2026/03/31 12:00:00 REQUEST: GET /api/health (1ms)

data: 2026/03/31 12:00:01 REQUEST: POST /api/chat (214ms)

200.description: Managed AI Apps and their local state.

post.description: Starts the long-running runtime for AI Apps that expose one, currently OpenClaw and CSGClaw.
Stops the long-running runtime for AI Apps that expose one, currently OpenClaw and CSGClaw.

post.summary: Open an AI App entry point
post.description: Returns a URL to the AI App web UI or shell entry after validating install state and model availability.

post.summary: Save preferred model for an AI App
post.description: Persists the user's preferred model choice for a given AI App so it is used as the default on next launch.

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: AI App identifier returned from `/api/apps`.

200.description: Recent and live AI App logs.

text/event-stream.example:
data: [install] starting openclaw

data: [install] download complete

get.summary: Attach to an AI App shell session
get.description: Upgrades the request to a WebSocket connection. The server sends JSON control messages such as `ready` and `exit`, plus binary frames containing terminal output. Clients may send binary input frames and JSON resize messages like `{ "type": "resize", "cols": 120, "rows": 36 }`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Shell session identifier returned by the AI App launch flow.

post.summary: Close an AI App shell session

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Shell session identifier returned by the AI App launch flow.

post.description: Compatibility layer for OpenAI Chat Completions. When `stream` is true the server streams SSE chunks and ends with `data: [DONE]`.

200.description: Chat completion output or streamed chunk sequence.

post.description: Compatibility layer for local text-to-image generation. The Diffusers runtime is checked lazily when this endpoint is called.

example.prompt: A watercolor cat reading a book

503.description: Diffusers runtime is not installed or not ready.

post.description: Compatibility layer for OpenAI Embeddings. Local models are served through llama-server in embedding mode.

200.description: Embedding vectors for the supplied input.

get.description: Returns an OpenAI model list by default. When Anthropic-style headers such as `anthropic-version` are present, the same route returns an Anthropic-compatible model list instead.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: Force a refresh of the cloud model catalog before returning results.

parameters.name: anthropic-version
parameters.description: Send this header to receive Anthropic-compatible model metadata.

parameters.name: anthropic-beta
parameters.description: Additional Anthropic compatibility hint that also switches the response shape.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Anthropic-style API key header. When provided without `authorization`, the route returns the Anthropic response format.

get.description: Returns a clear compatibility error for websocket probes and other unsupported GET requests to the Responses API endpoint.

426.description: WebSocket transport is not supported on this endpoint.

error.message: websocket transport is not supported on this endpoint

post.description: Minimal Responses API compatibility for Codex and OpenAI SDK clients. When `stream` is true the route emits Responses API-style SSE events and ends with `event: done` plus `[DONE]`.

200.description: Responses API output or streamed event sequence.

post.description: Anthropic Messages compatibility endpoint. When `stream` is true the route emits Anthropic event-stream events such as `message_start`, `content_block_delta`, and `message_stop`.

200.description: Anthropic-compatible message response or SSE event sequence.

messages.content: Count the tokens in this sentence.

post.description: OpenAI-compatible audio transcription endpoint served by the local ASR runtime.

BadRequest.description: The request body, query parameters, or path parameters were invalid.

Unauthorized.description: Authentication is required or the provided token is invalid.

NotFound.description: The requested model, dataset, app, or shell session was not found.

Conflict.description: The request conflicts with the current AI App state.

BadGateway.description: The upstream CSGHub service returned an error.

ServiceUnavailable.description: The requested streaming service is not currently available.

OpenAIBadRequest.description: The request was invalid for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

OpenAIUnauthorized.description: Authentication is required or invalid for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

OpenAITooManyRequests.description: The OpenAI-compatible endpoint rejected the request due to rate limiting.

OpenAIInternalError.description: The OpenAI-compatible endpoint encountered an internal error.

AnthropicBadRequest.description: The request was invalid for the Anthropic-compatible endpoint.

AnthropicUnauthorized.description: Authentication is required or invalid for the Anthropic-compatible endpoint.

AnthropicTooManyRequests.description: The Anthropic-compatible endpoint rejected the request due to rate limiting.

AnthropicInternalError.description: The Anthropic-compatible endpoint encountered an internal error.

UpgradeProgressEventStream.description: Server-Sent Event stream containing UpgradeProgressResponse JSON payloads.

source.description: Provider source, such as local, cloud, or provider.

id.description: Existing provider ID. When supplied, a blank API key reuses the saved key for validation.

model.description: Provider catalog model ID to select. This becomes the initial client-visible model ID and is retained internally as the original upstream model ID.

display_name.description: Optional custom display name shown in model lists. When omitted, the provider default name is used.

description.description: Optional custom model description shown in model metadata. When omitted, the provider default description is used.

model.description: New client-visible model ID. When the `model` query parameter is omitted, this value is used only to locate the existing model for metadata-only legacy requests.

display_name.description: Custom display name. Send an empty string to clear the override.

description.description: Custom model description. Send an empty string to clear the override.

cloud_provider_name.description: Provider name shown for built-in cloud models.

server_url.description: CSGHub marketplace server URL. Empty string restores the default server URL.

ai_gateway_url.description: OpenCSG AI Gateway URL. Empty string clears the custom value and uses the default AI Gateway.

cloud_provider_name.description: Provider name shown for built-in cloud models. Empty string restores the default provider name.

local_tokens.description: Total tokens consumed by local models.

cloud_tokens.description: Total tokens consumed by non-local models, including OpenCSG cloud and third-party providers.

total_history.description: Historical total tokens for the selected provider filter, independent of the selected period.

providers.description: Optional provider order. Empty means automatic order.

model.description: Model ID to run. Local models accept the short public ID returned by model list APIs; legacy `namespace/name` IDs are also accepted.
Model ID returned by the request.
Model ID to run. Local models accept the short public ID returned by model list APIs; legacy `namespace/name` IDs are also accepted.

source.description: Optional source hint. Use `cloud` to force an OpenCSG-backed chat model.

model.description: Model ID returned by the request.

quant.description: GGUF quantization when multiple weight variants exist (e.g. Q4_K_M). Ignored for non-GGUF models.

model.description: Model ID to delete. Local models accept the short public ID returned by model list APIs; legacy `namespace/name` IDs are also accepted.
Model ID to inspect. Local models accept the short public ID returned by model list APIs; legacy `namespace/name` IDs are also accepted.
Model ID to stop. Local models accept the short public ID returned by model list APIs; legacy `namespace/name` IDs are also accepted.
Model ID to load. Local models accept the short public ID returned by model list APIs; legacy `namespace/name` IDs are also accepted.

name.description: Client-visible model ID. Local models use a short public ID without `namespace/`; when local names collide, later models receive a stable hash suffix.

model.description: Client-visible model ID. Local models use a short public ID without `namespace/`; when local names collide, later models receive a stable hash suffix.

provider.description: Normalized provider name for filtering `/api/tags`, such as `local`, `csghub`, or a third-party provider name.

category.description: csghub-lite category derived from the model pipeline tag.

input_modalities.description: Input modalities supported by the model, such as text, image, video, audio, or file.

output_modalities.description: Output modalities produced by the model, such as text, image, video, audio, speech, or transcription.

has_more.description: Whether there are more results available

has_api_key.description: True when a manual OpenCSG API key is saved or a built-in API key is available for the authenticated user.

api_key_prefix.description: Redacted API key prefix for display only.

api_key_error.description: Error returned while resolving the built-in API key, if any.

source.description: Model source such as local, cloud, or provider:<id>.

work_dir.description: Working directory used for shell-based AI App launches.

runtime_supported.description: Whether this app exposes a long-running runtime that can be started or stopped.

runtime_running.description: Whether the app runtime is currently reachable.

full_name.description: Full local model repository ID when the marketplace model is downloaded.

public_id.description: Public local inference model ID when the marketplace model is downloaded.

upgrade_packages.description: Upgrade Diffuser-related Python packages instead of only installing missing runtime dependencies.

source.description: Model source such as local, cloud, or provider:<id>.

num_ctx.description: csghub-lite local runtime override for llama-server context length.

n_gpu_layers.description: csghub-lite local runtime override for llama-server GPU offload.

dtype.description: csghub-lite local SafeTensors to GGUF conversion dtype.

source.description: Model source such as local, cloud, or provider:<id>.

upgrade_packages.description: Upgrade ASR Python packages while installing.

hotwords.description: Comma-separated or JSON array hotwords for FunASR models.

itn.description: Enable inverse text normalization for supported ASR models.
